infobox.value: ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *Ordine dei Santi Maurizio e Lazzaro*) (abbreviated OSML) is a [Roman Catholic](/wiki/Roman_Catholic "Roman Catholic") [dynastic order of knighthood](/wiki/Dynastic_order_of_knighthood "Dynastic order of knighthood") bestowed by the royal [House of Savoy](/wiki/House_of_Savoy "House of Savoy"). It is the second-oldest order of knighthood in the world, tracing its lineage to AD 1119, and it is one of the rare orders of knighthood recognized by [papal bull](/wiki/Papal_bull "Papal bull"), in this case by [Pope Gregory XIII](/wiki/Pope_Gregory_XIII "Pope Gregory XIII").[[2]](#cite_note-2) In that bull, Pope Gregory XIII bestowed upon [Emmanuel Philibert, Duke of Savoy](/wiki/Emmanuel_Philibert,_Duke_of_Savoy "Emmanuel Philibert, Duke of Savoy") and his Savoy successors, the right to confer this knighthood in perpetuity. The [Grand Master](/wiki/Grand_master_(order) "Grand master (order)") is [Prince Emanuele Filiberto of Savoy, Prince of Venice](/wiki/Emanuele_Filiberto_of_Savoy,_Prince_of_Venice "Emanuele Filiberto of Savoy, Prince of Venice"), also known as the Duke of Savoy, the grandson of the last [King of Italy](/wiki/King_of_Italy "King of Italy"), [Umberto II](/wiki/Umberto_II_of_Italy "Umberto II of Italy"). However, Emanuele Filiberto's cousin twice removed [Prince Aimone, Duke of Aosta](/wiki/Aimone_di_Savoia_Aosta_(born_1967) "Aimone di Savoia Aosta (born 1967)") in theory claims to be the rightful grand master as [his father](/wiki/Prince_Amedeo,_Duke_of_Aosta_(1943%E2%80%932021) "Prince Amedeo, Duke of Aosta (1943–2021)") claimed to be head of the house of Savoy.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Roman Catholic dynastic order of knighthood

This article is about the dynastic chivalric order established in 1572. For the history of the medieval Catholic military order, see [Order of Saint Lazarus](/wiki/Order_of_Saint_Lazarus "Order of Saint Lazarus"). For the National Infantry Association award in the United States, see [Order of Saint Maurice (United States)](/wiki/Order_of_Saint_Maurice_(United_States) "Order of Saint Maurice (United States)").

The **Order of Saints Maurice and Lazarus** ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *Ordine dei Santi Maurizio e Lazzaro*) (abbreviated OSML) is a [Roman Catholic](/wiki/Roman_Catholic "Roman Catholic") [dynastic order of knighthood](/wiki/Dynastic_order_of_knighthood "Dynastic order of knighthood") bestowed by the royal [House of Savoy](/wiki/House_of_Savoy "House of Savoy"). It is the second-oldest order of knighthood in the world, tracing its lineage to AD 1119, and it is one of the rare orders of knighthood recognized by [papal bull](/wiki/Papal_bull "Papal bull"), in this case by [Pope Gregory XIII](/wiki/Pope_Gregory_XIII "Pope Gregory XIII").[[2]](#cite_note-2) In that bull, Pope Gregory XIII bestowed upon [Emmanuel Philibert, Duke of Savoy](/wiki/Emmanuel_Philibert,_Duke_of_Savoy "Emmanuel Philibert, Duke of Savoy") and his Savoy successors, the right to confer this knighthood in perpetuity. The [Grand Master](/wiki/Grand_master_(order) "Grand master (order)") is [Prince Emanuele Filiberto of Savoy, Prince of Venice](/wiki/Emanuele_Filiberto_of_Savoy,_Prince_of_Venice "Emanuele Filiberto of Savoy, Prince of Venice"), also known as the Duke of Savoy, the grandson of the last [King of Italy](/wiki/King_of_Italy "King of Italy"), [Umberto II](/wiki/Umberto_II_of_Italy "Umberto II of Italy"). However, Emanuele Filiberto's cousin twice removed [Prince Aimone, Duke of Aosta](/wiki/Aimone_di_Savoia_Aosta_(born_1967) "Aimone di Savoia Aosta (born 1967)") in theory claims to be the rightful grand master as [his father](/wiki/Prince_Amedeo,_Duke_of_Aosta_(1943%E2%80%932021) "Prince Amedeo, Duke of Aosta (1943–2021)") claimed to be head of the house of Savoy.

The order was formerly awarded by the [Kingdom of Italy](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Italy "Kingdom of Italy") (1861–1946) with the heads of the House of Savoy as the [Kings of Italy](/wiki/King_of_Italy "King of Italy"). Originally a [chivalric order](/wiki/Chivalric_order "Chivalric order") of noble nature, it was at one time restricted to members of noble families with proofs of eight noble great-grandparents[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]. The order's military and noble nature was and is still combined with a Roman Catholic character.

After the abolition of the monarchy and the foundation of the [Italian Republic](/wiki/Italian_Republic "Italian Republic") in 1946, the legacy of the order is maintained by the pretenders of the House of Savoy and the Italian throne in exile.

The order is estimated to include about 2,000 members around the world, with about 200 in the United States. The Order also has roster consultative status with the [United Nations](/wiki/United_Nations "United Nations"), as part of the U.N.'s [ECOSOC](/wiki/ECOSOC "ECOSOC").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

[Emmanuel Philibert, Duke of Savoy](/wiki/Emmanuel_Philibert,_Duke_of_Savoy "Emmanuel Philibert, Duke of Savoy") (1528–1580), founder and first [Grand Master General](/wiki/Grand_master_(order) "Grand master (order)") of the combined Order of Saints Maurice and Lazarus in recognition by Pope [Gregory XIII](/wiki/Gregory_XIII "Gregory XIII")

### Order of Saint Lazarus (1119)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Saints_Maurice_and_Lazarus&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Order of Saint Lazarus (1119)")]

Main article: [Order of Saint Lazarus](/wiki/Order_of_Saint_Lazarus "Order of Saint Lazarus")

The [Order of Saint Lazarus](/wiki/Order_of_Saint_Lazarus "Order of Saint Lazarus"), founded c. 1119, can be traced to the establishment around 1100, of a [hospital](/wiki/Hospital "Hospital") for [leprosy](/wiki/Leprosy "Leprosy") in [Jerusalem](/wiki/Jerusalem "Jerusalem"), [Kingdom of Jerusalem](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Jerusalem "Kingdom of Jerusalem"), by a group of [crusaders](/wiki/Crusades "Crusades") who called themselves "Brothers of Saint Lazarus".[[5]](#cite_note-Haileybury-5) Those knights protected [Christian](/wiki/Christian "Christian") [pilgrims](/wiki/Pilgrims "Pilgrims") to the [Holy Land](/wiki/Holy_Land "Holy Land"). From its inception, the order was concerned with the relief of leprosy and other illnesses, and many of its members were lepers who had been [knights](/wiki/Knight "Knight") in other orders. It became rich, its practices dubious, and its funds eventually abused. With the [fall of Acre](/wiki/Siege_of_Acre_(1291) "Siege of Acre (1291)") in 1291, the Knights of Saint Lazarus emigrated from the [Holy Land](/wiki/Holy_Land "Holy Land") and [Egypt](/wiki/Egypt "Egypt") and settled in [France](/wiki/France "France") and, in 1311, in [Naples](/wiki/Naples "Naples"). In the 16th century, the order declined in credibility and wealth. With papal support, the [Duke of Savoy](/wiki/Duke_of_Savoy "Duke of Savoy") became Grand Master in 1572. During medieval times, the Order of Saint Lazarus maintained a number of hospitals, including an institution in the Italian city of [Capua](/wiki/Capua "Capua").

### Order of Saint Maurice (1434)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Saints_Maurice_and_Lazarus&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Order of Saint Maurice (1434)")]

"Order of Saint Maurice" redirects here. For the US Army award, see [Order of Saint Maurice (United States)](/wiki/Order_of_Saint_Maurice_(United_States) "Order of Saint Maurice (United States)").

The Order of Saint Maurice was established in 1434 by [Amedeo VIII of Savoy](/wiki/Antipope_Felix_V "Antipope Felix V"), during his stay in the [Ripaglia](/wiki/Ripaglia "Ripaglia") hermitage near [Thonon](/wiki/Thonon "Thonon"), named after [Saint Maurice](/wiki/Saint_Maurice "Saint Maurice") of the [Theban Legion](/wiki/Theban_Legion "Theban Legion"). From its beginning, it was a [military order](/wiki/Military_order_(monastic_society) "Military order (monastic society)").[[5]](#cite_note-Haileybury-5) The order declined, but in 1572 was reestablished by [Pope Pius V](/wiki/Pope_Pius_V "Pope Pius V") at the instigation of the then-Duke of Savoy.

### Order of Saints Maurice and Lazarus (1572)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Saints_Maurice_and_Lazarus&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Order of Saints Maurice and Lazarus (1572)")]

In 1572, Pope [Gregory XIII](/wiki/Gregory_XIII "Gregory XIII") united the Order of Saint Lazarus in perpetuity with the Crown of Savoy. [Emmanuel Philibert, Duke of Savoy](/wiki/Emmanuel_Philibert,_Duke_of_Savoy "Emmanuel Philibert, Duke of Savoy"), merged it with the Savoyan Order of Saint Maurice, and thenceforth the title of Grand Master of the Order of Saints Maurice and Lazarus was hereditary in that house. The pope gave him authority over the vacant [commanderies](/wiki/Commandery "Commandery") everywhere, except in the states of the [King of Spain](/wiki/King_of_Spain "King of Spain"), I. e. the [Kingdoms of Naples](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Naples "Kingdom of Naples"), [Sicily](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Sicily "Kingdom of Sicily") and [Sardinia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Sardinia "Kingdom of Sardinia") and the [Duchy of Milan](/wiki/Duchy_of_Milan "Duchy of Milan"). In England and Germany, these commanderies were suppressed by the [Protestant reformation](/wiki/Protestant_reformation "Protestant reformation").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f3/BasilicaMaurizianaTorino.JPG/250px-BasilicaMaurizianaTorino.JPG)](/wiki/File:BasilicaMaurizianaTorino.JPG)

[Basilica Mauriziana](/wiki/Basilica_Mauriziana "Basilica Mauriziana") in [Turin](/wiki/Turin "Turin"), [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"), associated with the Order of Saints Maurice and Lazarus

The new organization was charged to defend the [Holy See](/wiki/Holy_See "Holy See") and Italian shores, as well as continue to assist the sick. The war [galleys](/wiki/Galley "Galley") of the order fought against the [Ottoman Empire](/wiki/Ottoman_Empire "Ottoman Empire") and the [Barbary pirates](/wiki/Barbary_pirates "Barbary pirates") with the [United States Marine Corps](/wiki/United_States_Marine_Corps "United States Marine Corps"). When leprosy again broke out, the order founded a hospital in [Aosta](/wiki/Aosta "Aosta") in 1773.

During the [Napoleonic Wars](/wiki/Napoleonic_Wars "Napoleonic Wars"), the order lost its estates in [Piedmont](/wiki/Piedmont "Piedmont") and accepted many officers of the [Royal Sardinian Army](/wiki/Royal_Sardinian_Army "Royal Sardinian Army") and [Navy](/wiki/Royal_Sardinian_Navy "Royal Sardinian Navy"), who in 1815 were bestowed with the [Military Order of Savoy](/wiki/Military_Order_of_Savoy "Military Order of Savoy").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/20/Carlo_Alberto_nelle_vesti_di_Gran_Maestro_dell%27Ordine_del_Mauriziano.png/250px-Carlo_Alberto_nelle_vesti_di_Gran_Maestro_dell%27Ordine_del_Mauriziano.png)](/wiki/File:Carlo_Alberto_nelle_vesti_di_Gran_Maestro_dell%27Ordine_del_Mauriziano.png)

King [Charles Albert of Sardinia](/wiki/Charles_Albert_of_Sardinia "Charles Albert of Sardinia") in ceremonial robe

In 1831, under [Charles Albert of Sardinia](/wiki/Charles_Albert_of_Sardinia "Charles Albert of Sardinia") the order was opened specifically for members of the upcoming [bourgeoisie](/wiki/Bourgeoisie "Bourgeoisie").

Brought back in favour by King [Victor Emmanuel II of Italy](/wiki/Victor_Emmanuel_II_of_Italy "Victor Emmanuel II of Italy"), the order was sparingly conferred for distinguished service in military and civilian affairs as an exclusive award compared with the more common [Order of the Crown of Italy](/wiki/Order_of_the_Crown_of_Italy "Order of the Crown of Italy"), consisting of five classes: [Knight Grand Cross](/wiki/Knight_Grand_Cross "Knight Grand Cross"), Knight Grand [Officer](/wiki/Officer "Officer"), [Knight Commander](/wiki/Commander_(order) "Commander (order)"), Knight Officer and [Knight](/wiki/Knight "Knight").[[5]](#cite_note-Haileybury-5)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/79/Greater_coat_of_arms_of_the_Kingdom_of_Italy_%281870-1890%29.svg/250px-Greater_coat_of_arms_of_the_Kingdom_of_Italy_%281870-1890%29.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Greater_coat_of_arms_of_the_Kingdom_of_Italy_(1870-1890).svg)

Great [coat of arms](/wiki/Coat_of_arms "Coat of arms") of Italy, including the insignia of the order

With the [Italian unification](/wiki/Italian_unification "Italian unification") (1860–1871), the order became a *de facto* Italian state [order](/wiki/Order_(distinction) "Order (distinction)").

After Italy became a republic in 1946, the order was effectively replaced by the government's [Order of Merit of the Italian Republic](/wiki/Order_of_Merit_of_the_Italian_Republic "Order of Merit of the Italian Republic"). Since 1951 it has not been officially recognized by the Italian Republic, but remains recognized by most other jurisdictions, particularly those with extant royal houses.

The formerly related [Maurician medal](/wiki/Maurician_medal "Maurician medal") for Military Merit of fifty years, established in 1839, was one of the few medals not suppressed by the Italian Republic, becoming the Maurician medal of Merit for 50 years military career in 1954.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/83/Victor_Emmanuel_of_Savoie_%280%29.jpg/250px-Victor_Emmanuel_of_Savoie_%280%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Victor_Emmanuel_of_Savoie_(0).jpg)

[Vittorio Emanuele, Prince of Naples](/wiki/Vittorio_Emanuele,_Prince_of_Naples "Vittorio Emanuele, Prince of Naples"), in the ceremonial robe of the Grand Master

The House of Savoy in exile continues to bestow the order on recipients eminent in the public service, science, art, letters, trade, and charitable works. While the continued use of those decorations conferred prior to 1951 is permitted in Italy, the crowns on the ribbons issued before 1946 must be substituted for as many five-pointed stars on military uniforms.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Organisation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Saints_Maurice_and_Lazarus&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Organisation")]

Until 1851, the knights of the order were divided into Knights of Justice, who had to prove nobility of eight great-grandparents, and Knights of Grace, who, like the [Knights of the Most Holy Annunciation](/wiki/Supreme_Order_of_the_Most_Holy_Annunciation "Supreme Order of the Most Holy Annunciation"), were "suspended from the proof" (not required to provide such proof of nobility).

All knights, regardless of category, were required to take three vows: obedience to the Grand Master; chastity except within marriage (castità almeno conjugale), meaning fidelity within marriage with papal approval; and service to the sick and poor.

In 1831, [Charles Albert of Sardinia](/wiki/Charles_Albert_of_Sardinia "Charles Albert of Sardinia") made the vow of the Knights of Grace facultative, and in 1851 the category of Justice was abolished by [Victor Emmanuel II](/wiki/Victor_Emmanuel_II "Victor Emmanuel II").

Today, women can be appointed as Dames, and a new category of Knights and Dames of Devotion was introduced in 1988. Also, the Grand Master can appoint hereditary *[jus patronatus](/wiki/Jus_patronatus "Jus patronatus") Commanders of Merit*.

### Grades

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Saints_Maurice_and_Lazarus&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Grades")]

Originally, the order was divided into Knights Petit Cross and Knights Grand Cross, the latter worn at the neck, the former on the chest. The *[Commendatori](/wiki/Commander_(order) "Commander (order)")*, who owned a [commandery](/wiki/Commandery "Commandery"), weren't bestowed with a specific insignia. In 1831, Charles Albert of Sardinia divided the order into three classes:

* Knight Grand Cross with the Grand Cordon
* Knight Commander
* Knight

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/74/Wearing_of_the_insignia_of_the_Order_of_Saints_Maurice_and_Lazarus.svg/250px-Wearing_of_the_insignia_of_the_Order_of_Saints_Maurice_and_Lazarus.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Wearing_of_the_insignia_of_the_Order_of_Saints_Maurice_and_Lazarus.svg)

Insignia of the grades

Since 1855, the Order is divided into five classes for the Knights (male members):[[8]](#cite_note-8)

* *Knight Grand Cordon*, who wear a sash on the right shoulder to the left hip and the badge as well as star are worn on the left side;
* *Grand Officer*, who wear a necklet plus the star on the left chest;
* *Commander*, who wear a necklet;
* *Officer*, who wear a medal in Gold on the left side of the chest;
* *Knight*, who wear a smaller Medal in Silver on the left side of the chest.

For female members the Order is divided into in three classes:

* *Dame Grand Cordon*, wearing a sash similar to that of their male counterparts
* *Dame Commander*, who wear on a necklet. During daydress: wear a bow-formed necklet worn on the left side of the chest. During evening wear: bow formed necklet worn on the left side of the chest)
* *Dame*, who wear a smaller necklet; During daydress and evening wear: bow formed medal worn on the left side of the chest

Special Class of the Order:

* *Knight Grand Cordon, Special Class*, For the Grand Master of the Order; who wear a sash on the right shoulder to the left hip, the badge as well as star which is worn on the left side of the stomach are in [Brilliants](/wiki/Brilliant_(diamond_cut) "Brilliant (diamond cut)")

### Council

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Saints_Maurice_and_Lazarus&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Council")]

The council is responsible for running the order, the order focuses mainly on charitable acts.[[9]](#cite_note-auto-9)

Until 1831, it was composed of the First Secretary of the Grand Magistracy (not to be confused with the Secretary of the Council itself), the Auditor General and seven dignitaries (*Grandati*):

* [Grand Prior](/wiki/Grand_Prior "Grand Prior"), a [clergyman](/wiki/Clergy "Clergy") with [episcopal rank](/wiki/Bishop "Bishop") as the
* [Grand Admiral](/wiki/Grand_Admiral "Grand Admiral"), the commander of the order's navy
* [Grand Marshal](/wiki/Grand_Marshal "Grand Marshal"), the commander of the order's land forces
* Grand Hospitaller, administrator of the order's hospitals
* Grand Conservator, administrator of the order's estates
* Grand Chancellor, head of the order's [chancery](/wiki/Chancery_(medieval_office) "Chancery (medieval office)")
* Grand [Treasurer](/wiki/Treasurer "Treasurer")

In 1831, the offices of Grand Admiral and Grand Marshal were abolished since the absence of an army. In 1851, the offices of Grand Conservator and Grand Chancellor where merged with the office of the First Secretary of the Grand Magistracy and the Grand Treasurer got renamed as Treasurer General, who also served as Vice Chairman of the council. The other nine members, among them an annual Chairman of the council, who replaced the Grand Prior, where chosen by the Grand master.

From 1861 to 1946, the council also served as the council of the [Order of the Crown of Italy](/wiki/Order_of_the_Crown_of_Italy "Order of the Crown of Italy").

Today, some of the old offices are re-established.

### Junta

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Saints_Maurice_and_Lazarus&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Junta")]

The Junta is responsible for voting in new [Knight](/wiki/Knight "Knight") or [Dame](/wiki/Dame "Dame") to the dynastic order on behalf of the grand master, [Emanuele Filiberto, Prince of Venice](/wiki/Emanuele_Filiberto,_Prince_of_Venice "Emanuele Filiberto, Prince of Venice"). There are always five members of the Junta to ensure that there is never an equal count in votes for and against a new possible new [Knight](/wiki/Knight "Knight") or [Dame](/wiki/Dame "Dame").[[9]](#cite_note-auto-9)
